national_expectations.summary: Croatia enter the Round of 32 carrying a remarkable recent pedigree (2018 runners-up, 2022 third place) yet are framed by major previews as outsiders this time, with the public mood treating progress as a bonus rather than a birthright. Coverage stresses a side in transition, no longer the team of 2018 or 2022, leaning on tournament savvy and Modric's leadership rather than overwhelming favouritism. The runners-up finish in Group L behind England met the immediate target of reaching the knockouts; against Portugal, Croatian and international press cast the tie as winnable but daunting, with Dalic himself tempering expectations.

national_mood.summary: The mood around the team is one of measured, resilient optimism rather than euphoria. After an opening defeat to England, the squad regrouped to grind out wins over Panama and Ghana, and reporting describes a cohesive dressing room. The dominant emotional thread is pride and affection for the ageless Modric, whose vintage display against Ghana lifted the team and the country, even as a Croatian outlet grumbles about the unfavourable middle-of-the-night kick-off time back home for the Portugal tie. Dalic explicitly tempered the celebrations, calling qualification only a small step.

manager_situation.summary: Zlatko Dalic, in charge since 2017, is leading Croatia at a third successive World Cup finals, having overseen the run to the 2018 final and the 2022 third-place finish. His standing is secure and his message after reaching the knockouts was deliberately low-key: he praised Modric lavishly, downplayed the achievement as only a small step, and insisted the identity of the next opponent was irrelevant because his team knows what it wants. He also acknowledged openly that this is Modric's last World Cup. Coverage portrays a manager with a close, trusting relationship with his veteran captain.

historical_context.summary: Croatia, a small nation, has punched far above its weight at World Cups, reaching the final at Russia 2018 (a 4-2 defeat to France) and finishing third at Qatar 2022 (a 2-1 win over Morocco in the playoff), after a third-place debut in 1998. Modric won the 2018 Golden Ball. The 2026 side reaches the knockout stage for a third World Cup in a row. In their two previous runs, Croatia repeatedly won knockout ties on penalties to reach the latter stages, a pedigree previews cite as a warning to opponents.

dominant_storylines.storyline: Modric vs Ronaldo as a likely final-curtain reunion: previews frame the tie as probably the last World Cup match for both 40-year-old Modric and Ronaldo, former Real Madrid teammates who won four Champions League titles together, lending the knockout a farewell, last-dance resonance.

The ageless Modric carrying a transitional Croatia: at 40 he produced a vintage display against Ghana, setting up Vlasic's late winner to become the oldest player on record to assist at a World Cup, and is repeatedly cast as the tonal axis and leader of a side blending veterans with a younger core like Petar Sucic.

Wily underdogs leaning on knockout experience: previews stress Croatia are no longer the team of 2018 or 2022 but remain savvy and content to play deliberately, with their recent record of winning knockout ties on penalties cited as a reason opponents should not relish facing them.

Dalic's public mea culpa over his tactics: in the Croatian press the manager openly admitted he set up the first two group matches wrong and took personal responsibility for the poorer displays, then framed the Ghana win as a return to the team's true identity after he reverted to a familiar shape. This self-critical, accountability-driven angle reframes the campaign as a coaching correction rather than a smooth progression.

The Euro 2016 grudge reawakened: Croatian outlets cast the Portugal tie as a rematch of the painful Euro 2016 last-16 defeat, when Ricardo Quaresma's late extra-time winner knocked Croatia out and Portugal went on to win the title. Reporting reopens that old wound for the home audience, lending the knockout a revenge subtext, and notes Portuguese paper A Bola also flagged the rematch.

Modric the 40-year-old defending, not just dictating: Croatian coverage dwelt on a vivid late-game image against Ghana in which the captain dropped back to left-back during the closing pressure and stopped 18-year-old Ernest Nuamah with a sliding tackle, with the home commentator calling him impenetrable that night in Philadelphia. The national press uses the moment to celebrate his sacrifice and stamina, broadening the leader narrative from his record assist to his graft in defence.

A daunting bracket and a human storyline behind the winning goal: national reporting frames Croatia's knockout path as brutal, potentially needing wins over top-tier opponents beyond Portugal, while also humanising the breakthrough through matchwinner Petar Sucic, who dedicated his goal to his pregnant wife and baby waiting at home. The pairing of a forbidding road ahead with an intimate dressing-room story captures the measured, emotionally invested mood in the Croatian press.

attributed_quotes.quote: The next opponent doesn't matter, we know what we can do and what we want.
attributed_quotes.context: After Croatia's 2-1 win over Ghana secured runners-up qualification from Group L, deliberately tempering expectations ahead of the knockouts.

attributed_quotes.quote: Luka was unbelievable today, I swear it seems like he tuned the time 10 years before.
attributed_quotes.context: Vlasic, who scored the late winner from Modric's assist against Ghana, praising the 40-year-old captain's performance.

attributed_quotes.quote: I set up the first two matches wrong, I took responsibility for the poorer performances.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: Krivo sam postavio prve dvije utakmice, preuzeo odgovornost za lošije nastupe.
attributed_quotes.context: After the 2-1 win over Ghana, Dalic publicly took the blame for his earlier team selections before the Portugal knockout tie.

attributed_quotes.quote: When we get past Portugal, then we'll talk about what's next.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: Kad prođemo Portugal, onda ćemo pričati što dalje.
attributed_quotes.context: Dalic keeping focus narrowly on the Portugal Round of 16 tie and refusing to look further ahead in the bracket.

attributed_quotes.quote: The manner in which the stoppage came angers me. An unnecessary foul.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: Ljuti me način na kojem je došlo do prekida. Nepotreban faul.
attributed_quotes.context: Dalic expressing frustration over the foul that led to Ghana's goal in the 2-1 win; verbatim excerpt of a longer sentence.

gaps: No verified injury, suspension, or lineup-availability reporting was found for any Croatia player ahead of the Portugal R32 tie; local_availability_reporting left empty rather than inventing doubts.

Exact match day/venue could not be pinned to a single authoritative FIFA source within this research (Sky Sports says Friday July 3 in Toronto; the Croatian outlet implies a late Thursday/Friday local kick-off); no betting or unofficial source was used to force precision.

No 'official' (FIFA/UEFA/HNS federation) fact-grade source was successfully fetched; the FIFA squad URL returned no content and was dropped, so all sections are tagged discovery / attributed_context_only.

A widely circulated Dalic line in gol.dnevnik.hr ('Ako prodemo Portugal, napravit cemo veliku stvar') was confirmed to be editorial paraphrase, not a verbatim quotation, so it was excluded from attributed_quotes; the outlet contains no direct verbatim quotes.
No direct verbatim quote from captain Luka Modric himself was found in the credible sources reviewed (coverage quoted teammates and the coach about him, not him); none invented.
Specific squad fitness notes (e.g., a pre-tournament Gvardiol recovery reference) were not re-verified as current for the Portugal match, so excluded from availability reporting.

All DB FACTS cross-checked and confirmed exactly: Croatia 2nd in Group L on 6 pts (England 4-2 Croatia L, Panama 0-1 Croatia W, Croatia 2-1 Ghana W = W2 L1), Modric captain, opponent Portugal; no contradictions found.

source_data_snapshot.used_for: Verbatim Dalic quotes (mea culpa on first two matches, focus on Portugal, anger over the foul that led to Ghana's goal); tactical-correction storyline. Re-fetched and confirmed verbatim.

Modric dropping to left-back and sliding-tackling 18-year-old Nuamah; commentator 'neprelazan/impenetrable' line; 'prava Hrvatska' framing; daunting knockout path; Sucic dedicating his goal to his pregnant wife and baby. Re-fetched and confirmed.

Euro 2016 'painful memory' rematch angle (Quaresma's 117th-minute winner), A Bola reaction. Re-fetched; A Bola line confirmed as paraphrase, not quoted verbatim. NOTE: this source does NOT support a broad all-time H2H 'poor record' claim (only the Euro 2016 reference).

DROPPED as a citation: page returned HTTP 403 on re-fetch and could not be verified; the Modric-Ronaldo legends-duel angle already exists in the file and the 'brutal draw' framing is independently sourced to Index.hr 2805614.
